assertion_results.text: The plan determines feature-branch workflow mode (not direct-to-main) based on the atomicity constraints in the feature description
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md line 5: 'Selected mode: feature-branch'. Rationale cites three atomicity indicators: coordinated schema migrations, breaking API changes, tightly coupled feature components. Directly quotes the feature's NFR: 'All changes must land together: merging the migration without the code changes would break all advisory queries, and merging the code changes without the migration would reference a column that does not exist.' summary-comment.md confirms 'Feature-branch (workflow:feature-branch label applied to TC-9005)'.

assertion_results.text: A bookend task with Bookend Type create-branch is generated as the first task in the plan
assertion_results.evidence: task-1-create-branch.md contains '## Bookend Type\ncreate-branch' and is Task 1. summary-comment.md table shows Task 1 as 'Create feature branch TC-9005 from main' with Type 'Bookend (create-branch)'.

assertion_results.text: A bookend task with Bookend Type merge-branch is generated as the last task in the plan
assertion_results.evidence: task-8-merge-branch.md contains '## Bookend Type\nmerge-branch' and is Task 8, the last task. summary-comment.md table shows Task 8 as 'Merge feature branch TC-9005 to main' with Type 'Bookend (merge-branch)'.

assertion_results.text: All intermediate tasks (between the create-branch and merge-branch bookend tasks) have Target Branch set to TC-9005
assertion_results.evidence: All six intermediate tasks have '## Target Branch\nTC-9005': task-2-advisory-status-migration.md line 5, task-3-update-entity-definitions.md line 5, task-4-update-advisory-service-endpoints.md line 5, task-5-update-ingestion-pipeline.md line 5, task-6-update-integration-tests.md line 5, task-7-update-documentation.md line 5.

assertion_results.text: The create-branch bookend task has Target Branch set to main
The merge-branch bookend task has Target Branch set to main

All non-documentation intermediate tasks list the create-branch bookend task as a dependency. Documentation tasks may list implementation tasks as dependencies instead of the create-branch bookend task, since documentation depends on implementation completion
assertion_results.evidence: Non-documentation tasks all list Task 1: Task 2 deps: 'Depends on: Task 1'; Task 3 deps: 'Depends on: Task 1' and 'Task 2'; Task 4 deps: 'Depends on: Task 1' and 'Task 3'; Task 5 deps: 'Depends on: Task 1' and 'Task 3'; Task 6 deps: 'Depends on: Task 1', 'Task 4', and 'Task 5'. Documentation task 7 lists Tasks 4, 5, and 6 as dependencies (no Task 1), which is allowed for documentation tasks.

assertion_results.text: The merge-branch bookend task lists all non-documentation intermediate tasks as dependencies
assertion_results.evidence: task-8-merge-branch.md lists dependencies on Tasks 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 (all non-documentation intermediate tasks). It also lists Task 7 (documentation), which is extra but does not violate the assertion that all non-doc tasks must be listed.

assertion_results.text: Each non-documentation task file contains all required template sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, at least one of Files to Modify or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Documentation tasks are exempt from requiring Files to Modify, Files to Create, and Implementation Notes — they must still include Repository, Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements
assertion_results.evidence: Bookend tasks (1, 8) have Repository, Target Branch, Bookend Type, Description, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements, Dependencies — exempt from Files to Modify/Create, Implementation Notes per grading rules. Implementation tasks 2-6 each have: Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Modify and/or Files to Create, Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements. Documentation task 7 has Repository, Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements — exempt from Files to Modify/Create and Implementation Notes.

assertion_results.text: The plan includes a workflow:feature-branch label decision to be applied to the feature issue
assertion_results.evidence: impact-map.md line 19: 'Label: The workflow:feature-branch label will be applied to the feature issue TC-9005.' Lines 76-78 detail the label update: 'Updated labels: ["ai-generated-jira", "workflow:feature-branch"]'. summary-comment.md line 4: 'Feature-branch (workflow:feature-branch label applied to TC-9005)'.

assertion_results.text: For each task created, a description digest is produced — evidenced by a separate digest file (e.g., task-N-digest.md), a digest entry in the impact map, or a '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' marker in any output file. The digest must contain a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string
assertion_results.evidence: Eight separate digest files exist (task-1-digest.md through task-8-digest.md), one per task. Each contains '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-hex-chars>'. Verified all hashes are 64 lowercase hex characters: task-1: 018d929e...debe51, task-2: 8518a6c8...de6a, task-3: 77ba905e...2951, task-4: d47f20d9...9e40, task-5: 0fc0e731...eb63, task-6: 7a88b2d6...3258, task-7: f319b947...ef61, task-8: 0ee71b22...e17.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: All convention references in task files follow the prescribed format. Task 2: two conventions with 'Applies: task creates migration/src/m0002_advisory_status_enum/mod.rs matching the convention's migration file scope' and '...matching the convention's .rs file scope'. Task 3: 'Applies: task modifies entity/src/advisory.rs matching the convention's .rs file scope'. Task 4: three conventions referencing specific .rs files. Task 5: 'Applies: task modifies modules/ingestor/src/graph/advisory/mod.rs matching the convention's .rs file scope'. Task 6: 'Applies: task modifies tests/api/advisory.rs matching the convention's .rs test file scope'. No inapplicable conventions listed with 'Not applicable' annotations. No free-form prose rationales found.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a priority set (not 'Undefined'), the plan documents that the Feature's priority is inherited by created tasks — evidenced by priority field values in the impact map, task creation log, or task description files. When the Feature's priority is 'Undefined', the plan documents that priority inheritance is skipped (not propagated as null or 'Undefined')
assertion_results.evidence: Feature TC-9005 has Priority: High. impact-map.md lines 64-68 show additional_fields JSON with '"priority": {"name": "High"}' and line 71: 'Priority: inherited from Feature TC-9005 (High)'. summary-comment.md line 29: 'Priority: High — inherited from Feature TC-9005 and propagated to all created tasks.'

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a non-empty fixVersions array and the fixVersion scope config (from ### Jira Field Defaults in CLAUDE.md) is 'task' or 'both' (or absent, defaulting to 'both'), the plan documents that fixVersions are inherited by created tasks — evidenced by fixVersion values in the impact map, task creation log, or task description files. When fixVersion scope is 'feature' or the Feature has no fixVersions, the plan documents that fixVersion inheritance is skipped
assertion_results.evidence: Feature TC-9005 has Fix Versions: RHTPA 2.0.0. No ### Jira Field Defaults section in CLAUDE.md, so fixVersion scope defaults to 'both'. impact-map.md line 67: '"fixVersions": [{"name": "RHTPA 2.0.0"}]' in additional_fields JSON. Line 72: 'fixVersions: inherited from Feature TC-9005 (RHTPA 2.0.0); fixVersion scope not configured in Jira Field Defaults, defaulting to "both" (propagate to tasks)'. summary-comment.md line 30: 'fixVersions: RHTPA 2.0.0 — inherited from Feature TC-9005 and propagated to all created tasks.'

assertion_results.text: A summary of inherited field propagation is produced — evidenced by a summary-comment.md file in the outputs directory, or a summary section in the impact map that includes the inherited priority and fixVersion values propagated to tasks, or states they were omitted and why
assertion_results.evidence: summary-comment.md exists and contains an '## Inherited Field Values' section (lines 28-30) documenting: 'Priority: High — inherited from Feature TC-9005 and propagated to all created tasks' and 'fixVersions: RHTPA 2.0.0 — inherited from Feature TC-9005 and propagated to all created tasks.' Additionally, impact-map.md 'Task Creation Details' section (lines 59-72) documents both fields with inheritance rationale.
